channel.title: NYT > World News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Zelensky Dismisses Ukraine’s Top General After Protests
item.description: The move followed the firing of a popular defense minister, who had clashed with the general over how to fight the war.
item.dc:creator: Marc Santora
item.media:credit: Brendan Hoffman for The New York Times
item.media:description: Volodymyr Zelensky and the Ukrainian commander in chief, Oleksandr Syrskyi, in Ukraine’s Donetsk region in March.

item.title: Inside the Fight to Rid South Africa of Black Immigrants
item.description: As immigration surges in the country, activists are going to extreme lengths to expel foreign nationals, often taking the law into their own hands.
item.dc:creator: John Eligon, Zimasa Matiwane and Joao Silva

item.title: 3 Myths Fueling Anti-Immigrant Sentiment in South Africa
item.description: As the nation convulses with protests and violence, data and research suggest that some of the biggest arguments against foreigners don’t stand up against the facts.
item.dc:creator: John Eligon
item.media:description: Supporters of the March and March movement, one of the main anti-immigrant organizations in South Africa, demonstrating in Johannesburg, in April.

item.title: India’s Young Protesters Are Bloodied but Unbowed
item.description: A day after police forcibly broke up demonstrations, thousands returned to the protest site in New Delhi to keep pushing for action over the country’s failings in education.
item.dc:creator: Pragati K.B., Anupreeta Das and Alex Travelli
item.media:description: Injured protesters at the main protest site in New Delhi on Tuesday. They have vowed to fight on for demands that include the resignation of India’s education minister.

item.title: Netanyahu Ad Uses a Questionable Tactic Against Bereaved Rival
item.description: A.I.-generated images showing Gadi Eisenkot, the Israeli leader’s top challenger in upcoming elections, played on anti-Arab sentiments and used images seen as disrespectful to his son, who was killed in the recent Gaza war.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger
item.media:description: Gadi Eisenkot, leader of the Yashar party, speaking at the launch of his party’s election campaign in Drom HaSharon, Israel, last month.

item.title: British Politician Ann Widdecombe Was Murdered with a Hammer, Prosecutors Say
item.description: The politician, Ann Widdecombe, was eating lunch at home when a man walked in and asked whether she had bank cards, security footage showed.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.media:description: The British former Prime Minister Liz Truss leads a tribute to the slain politician Ann Widdecombe in London last week.

item.title: Trump Escalates Canada Tariffs as Mark Carney Holds Firm
item.description: The president’s new tariffs on a top U.S. trading partner aim at gaining concessions, but the Canadian government, and the public, have been bracing for the escalation.
item.dc:creator: Matina Stevis-Gridneff
item.media:description: Steel coils at the Algoma Steel facility in Ontario in July. U.S. tariffs on goods such as steel, aluminum and vehicles have hit sensitive Canadian sectors.

item.title: Houthis in Yemen Edge Closer to Entering U.S.-Iran War
item.description: The Iran-backed Houthi militia in Yemen has declared a Red Sea blockade on Saudi Arabia, potentially opening a new front in the war rippling across the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Ismaeel Naar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Yemeni supporters of the Iran-backed Houthi movement gather during a rally against what the group says are restrictions imposed by the Saudi-led coalition on Houthi-controlled areas of Yemen, in Sana last week.

item.title: France Joins a Global Move Toward Restricting Social Media for Children
item.description: Its new law, the first of its kind enacted by a European country, will ban access to some accounts for anyone under 15.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler

item.title: The Teenage Boys In Search of Their Mother After the Venezuela Earthquake
item.description: After Venezuela’s devastating earthquakes, Winder and Deivi Delfín dug every day for 26 days to find the bodies of their mother, brother and grandmother.
item.dc:creator: Julie Turkewitz and Adriana Loureiro Fernandez
item.media:credit: Adriana Loureiro Fernandez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Winder Delfín, 17, right, and his brother Deivi, 15, during the 24th day of searching for their mother, younger brother and grandmother, who lived on the first floor of a twelve story building that collapsed during the twin earthquakes last month.

item.title: When Protesters Speak, Zelensky Listens, in the Ukrainian Tradition
item.description: With anger high over the firing of the defense minister, the president’s office is signaling an openness to compromise.
item.dc:creator: Andrew E. Kramer
item.media:description: A photograph released by the Ukrainian Presidential Press Service on Monday showing President Volodymyr Zelensky meeting with Gen. Mykhailo Drapaty, the commander of the Joint Forces, in Kyiv.

item.title: A Family’s Daring Escape From North Korea
item.description: Their resolve for freedom hardened during the pandemic, when they lived through a crippling food shortage and were forced to witness frequent public executions.
item.dc:creator: Choe Sang-Hun
item.media:credit: Woohae Cho for The New York Times
item.media:description: Kim Yu-mi, right, defected with a group that included her two children and her mother. Life in South Korea has not been quite what they imagined, but they harbor no regrets from deserting the North’s brutal dictatorship and relentless propaganda.

item.title: New UK Prime Minister Burnham Promises Hope and Change. The Hurdles Are High.
item.description: Britain’s new prime minister, Andy Burnham, promised policies that would give average Britons financial “breathing space.” His opponents are already doling out criticism.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.category: Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
item.media:description: Prime Minister Andy Burnham speaking in front of 10 Downing Street on Monday. “We will make this moment a circuit breaker for Britain,” he said.

item.title: Philippines Says Sailor Was Injured in Skirmish With Chinese Coast Guard
item.description: China said it was not the aggressor in the episode, which was the latest flare-up in the South China Sea between the two countries.
item.dc:creator: Max Kim
item.media:description: Philippine Navy personnel and China Coast Guard personnel clashing near a grounded Philippine warship at Second Thomas Shoal, in the South China Sea, on Monday.

item.title: Japan Is Startled by Prime Minister’s Grueling Sleep Schedule
item.description: Sanae Takaichi has said she gets just “zero to three hours” of sleep a night, prompting a debate in the country over work-life balance.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández and Hisako Ueno
item.media:description: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi at a meeting at Japan’s Parliament in Tokyo last week. A social media post by Ms. Takaichi about her schedule has prompted a debate about work hours and the hazards of sleep deprivation.

item.title: Iran’s Neighbors Long for a Deal, Any Deal, to End the War
item.description: Business owners in Pakistan are getting impatient for a chance to finally trade goods and oil along their long shared border with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier and Asim Hafeez
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Asim Hafeez for The New York Times

item.title: With His Latest Threats, Trump Signals to Iran That He Is Willing to Escalate
item.description: President Trump is facing a critical moment in the war, which has failed to bring about the “unconditional surrender” he once demanded.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: “We’ll be hitting that area probably pretty soon,” President Trump said of Pickaxe Mountain on Tuesday. “And there’s not a thing they can do about it.”

item.title: Mykhailo Drapatyi: What to Know About Ukraine’s New Top General
item.description: Maj. Gen. Mykhailo Drapatyi, a decorated commander, is popular both inside and outside of his country.
item.dc:creator: Cassandra Vinograd
item.media:description: Maj. Gen. Mykhailo Drapatyi speaking to journalists in Ukraine last year.

item.title: A Wartime Rift in Ukraine
item.description: President Volodymyr Zelensky fired his top general after a feud between old-school officers and younger innovators spilled into the streets.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.media:credit: Brendan Hoffman for The New York Times
item.media:description: Volodymyr Zelensky and the former commander in chief, Oleksandr Syrskyi, in March.

item.title: What to Know About Iran’s Pickaxe Mountain
item.description: The fortified underground complex, in central Iran, is about one mile away from the Natanz uranium enrichment facility.
item.dc:creator: Anushka Patil
item.category: Institute for Science and International Security
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A satellite image provided by Vantor shows an entrance to tunnels at Pickaxe Mountain in Iran last month.

item.title: Water Plants Under Fire in the Gulf Are Crucial to Civilians
item.description: Several days of airstrikes have hit water and energy plants in Kuwait, a nation that relies on desalination for 90 percent of its drinking water.
item.dc:creator: Zane Irwin
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Airbus DS SPOT CNES, via Reuters
item.media:description: A satellite image showing smoke rising from the Subiya Power and Water Desalination Plant in Kuwait on Saturday.

item.title: Iran’s top diplomat says ‘security gap’ may have led to strikes on supreme leader.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Abbas Araghchi, Iran’s foreign minister, during a news conference in New Delhi in May.

item.title: 1,700 H.I.V. Treatment Sites Closed After Trump Aid Cuts, a Study Finds
item.description: New research also shows that children and high-risk adult populations have been particularly affected.
item.dc:creator: Apoorva Mandavilli
item.category: amfAR, The Foundation for AIDS Research
President's Emergency Plan for AIDS Relief
item.media:description: A health clinic in Johannesburg that was closed in 2025, following cuts in U.S. aid.

item.title: Lebanese Army Accuses Israeli Troops of Firing as Its Forces Deploy in ‘Pilot Zone’
item.description: The action raised tensions as the sides were taking their first steps under a U.S.-brokered deal to transfer control of occupied areas to Lebanon after months of war.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Lebanese army troops in Zawtar al-Gharbiya, southern Lebanon, after Israeli forces withdrew from the area on Tuesday.

item.title: Flash Floods Kill at Least 23 in Afghanistan
item.description: Dozens remained missing after floods swept through a city in Nuristan, an isolated province in the country’s east.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier and Safiullah Padshah
item.media:description: A damaged bridge and building in the eastern province of Nuristan in Afghanistan on Tuesday. Heavy monsoon rains sent torrents of water down surrounding mountains.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Max Bearak, Jenny Gross, John Ismay and Catie Edmondson

item.title: Burnham, UK’s New Prime Minister, Starts With Electricity Tax Cut
item.description: Prime Minister Andy Burnham moved quickly to confront the high cost of living. And President Trump wished him well after a phone call between the two leaders.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.media:description: Prime Minister Andy Burnham of Britain outside No. 10 Downing Street on Monday. In a statement, Mr. Burnham said that he wanted “to give people breathing space.”

item.title: Australian Woman Gives Birth to ‘Miracle’ Identical Quadruplets
item.description: Doctors said the arrival of Emily, Harriet, Catherine and Alexa, who were conceived naturally and split from one fertilized egg, is an extremely rare event.
item.dc:creator: Laura Chung

item.title: At Least 27 Dead in Guyana After Ferry Capsizes
item.description: Dozens of others are feared dead as crews scour for bodies after the vessel sank on its way to a gold-mining outpost in the South American country.
item.dc:creator: Simon Romero
item.media:description: A handout image showing rescue efforts on Sunday after a ferry carrying scores of people capsized off the coast of Guyana in South America.

item.title: Daniel Ortega, President of Nicaragua, Says He Plans to Abolish Elections
item.description: Daniel Ortega has been accused of rigging recent elections in Nicaragua. Now, the authoritarian leader says the nation won’t even hold a vote.
item.dc:creator: Jack Nicas and James Wagner
item.media:description: President Daniel Ortega, 80, who with his wife has exerted complete control over Nicaragua for many years, at a conference in 2014.

item.title: Pope Leo Calls for Nuclear Disarmament in Latest Antiwar Message
item.description: In a new book published on Tuesday, Leo said that a race to nuclear de-escalation has been “obscured by a race to armament that today frightens and terrifies.”
item.dc:creator: Motoko Rich
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament
item.media:description: Pope Leo XIV has been a steady critic of the U.S.-Israeli war in Iran.

item.title: U.K. Prosecutors File Murder Charge in Ann Widdecombe Death
item.description: A 28-year-old man has been charged in connection with the death of Ann Widdecombe, a right-wing figure whose body was found in her home this month.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.media:description: Ann Widdecombe in 2024.

item.title: Epidemics and Rage
item.description: Societies have always struggled with hostile reactions to health care workers during epidemics. Why?
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arlette Bashizi for The New York Times

item.title: Evacuees of Ontario’s Wildfires Wait and Worry
item.description: Flames crept, and then sprinted, toward unsuspecting residents who fled remote parts of northwestern Ontario. Now they want to know why officials weren’t prepared.
item.dc:creator: Vjosa Isai and Cole Burston
item.media:credit: Cole Burston for The New York Times
item.media:description: Lyndon Paavola showing footage on his phone from his evacuation alongside community members from Namaygoosisagagun First Nation due to fast spreading wildfire that destroyed his community.

item.title: In Gaza, Spain’s Win Brings a Moment of Joy and Distraction
item.description: Many in the enclave supported Spain, which has recognized Palestinian statehood. Argentina had strong support among Israeli soccer fans.
item.dc:creator: Bilal Shbair and Claire Moses
item.media:description: Stringers Hub, via Associated Press

item.title: Inside Andy Burnham’s 1st Day as U.K. Prime Minister
item.description: As Andy Burnham became Britain’s seventh leader in a decade on Monday, he tried to show that he will govern differently.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle, Michael D. Shear and Megan Specia
item.media:description: King Charles III welcoming Mr. Burnham at Buckingham Palace.

item.title: Trump Says Netanyahu Will Not Be Arrested in the U.S.
item.description: New York’s mayor, Zohran Mamdani, told The New York Times, he was looking into whether he could arrest Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu when he comes to New York for the U.N. General Assembly.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
item.media:description: Israel’s ambassador to the United Nations, Danny Danon, accused New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani of engaging in “political theater” over his comments about arresting Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.

item.title: In Venezuela’s Rubble, a Search for the Family Pets
item.description: Volunteers are reuniting pets with families, offering Venezuelans comfort amid loss and devastation.
item.dc:creator: Tibisay Romero and Genevieve Glatsky
item.media:description: A firefighter rescuing a dog from a building in Caracas that collapsed after twin earthquakes last month.

item.title: Inside the Deadly U.S. Airstrike on a Shadow Fleet Tanker
item.description: Evidence suggests the ship on which three Indian sailors died was not moving when it was struck in the Gulf of Oman, but it had been engaged in risky offshore transfers of oil.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar, Pragati K.B. and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Outcry in Italy After Man Dies While Being Restrained by Police
item.description: The death of the man, a Moroccan resident of Italy, set off an outcry across the country and prompted comparisons to the death of George Floyd in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Motoko Rich and Josephine de La Bruyère
item.media:description: People gathered near the site where Abderrahim Fakir died on Sunday in Bologna, Italy. The death drew comparisons to the killing of George Floyd.

item.title: Suspected Iranian strikes have targeted the U.S. military in at least 4 countries.
item.dc:creator: Sanjana Varghese, Samuel Granados, Riley Mellen and Aric Toler

item.title: Hamas Names Khalil al-Hayya as Its New Leader
item.description: Seen as closer to Iran, Khalil al-Hayya served as the Palestinian militant group’s chief negotiator with Israel and the Trump administration during the Gaza war.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman and Adam Rasgon
item.media:description: Khalil al-Hayya speaking with reporters in Istanbul in 2024.

item.title: Houthis in Yemen Declare Red Sea Blockade on Saudi Arabia
item.description: The Iran-backed Houthi militia, which controls parts of Yemen, has in the past blockaded the Red Sea by threatening a choke point crucial to Saudi oil exports and global shipping.
item.dc:creator: Ismaeel Naar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Spain Celebrates Both a Soccer Win and a Sense of Wider Success
item.description: For many fans, the World Cup victory cemented a belief that something promising — a cultural, economic and sports revival — is happening in the country.
item.dc:creator: Jason Horowitz
item.media:credit: Joseph Fox for The New York Times
item.media:description: Spain fans watching the World Cup final in Plaza de Colón in Madrid on Sunday night.

item.title: What Happens Behind the Scenes Once Burnham Enters 10 Downing Street
item.description: Calls with foreign leaders, cabinet appointments and writing the “letters of last resort”: Andy Burnham’s first hours and days in office will be busy.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.media:description: The staff at 10 Downing Street welcoming new Prime Minister Andy Burnham and his wife Marie-France van Heel on Monday.

item.title: Aiming for My Fastest Marathon, I Turned to Distance Running’s Mecca
item.description: A New York Times reporter’s quest to beat the personal best he set 14 years ago took him to a Kenyan village, where the biggest benefit he got was not what he expected.
item.dc:creator: John Eligon and Brian Otieno
item.media:description: The author, in the middle in red, training in Iten, Kenya.

item.title: Burnham Set to Become Britain’s First Openly Roman Catholic Prime Minister
item.description: Andy Burnham has spoken of Catholicism as part of his cultural identity rather than as a fervent religious faith.
item.dc:creator: Megan Specia
item.media:description: Andy Burnham told an interviewer in 2009: “Three things are important in my life, apart from family. Everton F.C., the Labour Party and the Catholic church — in that order.”

item.title: Zelensky Faces Pressure to Remove Ukraine’s Top General
item.description: Demonstrators and many soldiers want President Volodymyr Zelensky to reaffirm the vision of drone warfare laid out by the defense minister he fired last week.
item.dc:creator: Marc Santora, Liubov Sholudko and Stanislav Kozliuk
item.media:credit: Jędrzej Nowicki for The New York Times
item.media:description: Protesters in Kyiv, Ukraine, last week criticized President Volodymyr Zelensky’s decision to dismiss Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov.

item.title: North Sea Drilling Offers First Test for Burnham’s Relationship With Trump
item.description: Andy Burnham has not yet met President Trump, but the two men spoke shortly after Mr. Burnham became prime minister.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.media:description: President Donald Trump departing Joint Base Andrews, Md., en route to New York, on Friday.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman, Ismaeel Naar and Sanam Mahoozi

item.title: Ukraine War Momentum Helps Spur Interest in Postwar Investment
item.description: At a conference on reconstruction, the focus shifted to private investment, not donor assistance.
item.dc:creator: Andrew E. Kramer
item.media:description: The Ukraine Recovery Conference in Gdansk, Poland, in June. The overarching pitch was to put money into Ukraine even as it remains at war.

item.title: Rubio Says That U.S. Is Still Open to Diplomacy With Iran
item.description: Iranian foreign ministry officials and Secretary of State Marco Rubio signaled openness to resume negotiations but declined to provide any new details.
item.dc:creator: Yan Zhuang
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Brendan Smialowski/Agence France-Presse, Pool/AFP via Getty Images
item.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio speaking to reporters at Joint Base Andrews in Maryland on Sunday night.

item.title: Police in India Use Tear Gas and Batons on Crowds Protesting Exam Scandal
item.description: Young people angry about frequent leaks of college entrance tests present one of the biggest challenges in years to Prime Minister Narendra Modi.
item.dc:creator: Mujib Mashal and Pragati K.B.
item.media:description: The police confronting crowds in New Delhi who had gathered before a planned march toward Parliament on Monday.
